exampleSentences: Khi vào thư viện, anh nhớ phải nói nhỏ để không làm phiền người khác.
Cô giáo yêu cầu cả lớp nói nhỏ trong giờ học.
Em có thể nói nhỏ với chị về chuyện này được không?
exampleSentencesTranslated: When entering the library, remember to speak quietly so you don't disturb others.
The teacher asks the whole class to speak quietly during the lesson.
Can you speak quietly with me about this matter?
notes:
nói nhỏ = to speak quietly, in a low voice. It’s about lowering your volume so others don’t hear clearly or you don’t disturb people.

Used when you want privacy, secrecy, or politeness in quiet places. Often goes with với (to someone) or về (about something).

Strong contrast: nói to (speak loudly). You can remember it as the opposite of raising your voice.
notes_md:
**nói nhỏ** = to speak quietly, in a low voice. It’s about lowering your volume so others don’t hear clearly or you don’t disturb people.

Used when you want privacy, secrecy, or politeness in quiet places. Often goes with **với** (to someone) or **về** (about something).


Strong contrast: **nói to** (speak loudly). You can remember it as the opposite of raising your voice.
